structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Enable/Disable Based on System Status
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable or disable control loops based on system status to prevent windup.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Initialization on Enable/Reenable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a control loop is enabled or reenabled, it and all its constituents shall be set initially to a neutral value.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set the loop and its constituents (proportional and integral terms) to a neutral value.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Neutral Control Loop Condition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect (valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply minimum control effect: valves/dampers closed, VFDs at minimum speed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Selection
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Systems with multiple outdoor air temperature sensors
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When there are multiple outdoor air temperature sensors, the system shall use the valid sensor that most accurately represents the outdoor air conditions at the equipment being controlled.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Multiple outdoor air temperature sensors available
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Use the valid sensor that most accurately represents outdoor air conditions at the equipment.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Validity for Air-Handler Intakes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when the supply fan is proven ON and the unit is in Occupied Mode or in any other mode with the economizer enabled.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan proven ON and unit in Occupied Mode or economizer enabled
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Consider sensor valid only under those conditions.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Outdoor Air Temperature Averaging for Global Sequences
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Global sequences (optimum start, plant lockout, etc.)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid sensors, discard the highest and lowest readings.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Global sequence requiring outdoor air temperature
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Average all valid sensor readings; if four or more valid sensors, discard highest and lowest before averaging.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The outdoor air temperature used for optimum start, plant lockout, and other global sequences shall be the average of all valid sensor readings. If there are four or more valid outdoor air temperature sensors, discard the highest and lowest temperature readings.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Proportional-Only Loops for Limiting Loops
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Limiting loops (e.g., zone CO2 control loops)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Use proportional-only (P-only) loops for limiting loops to prevent integral windup from causing overshoot.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Derivative Term Usage Restriction
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Do not use the derivative term on any loops unless field tuning is not possible without it.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Control Loop Output Rate of Change Limit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Limit output rate of change; default 25% per minute.
configurable_values: Maximum rate of change (default 25% per minute)

structured_payload_candidate.title: Adjustability of Setpoints, Timers, Deadbands, PID Gains
structured_payload_candidate.summary: All setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains, etc. listed in sequences shall be adjustable by the user with appropriate access level. Software points shall be used; fixed scalar numbers shall not be embedded except for physical constants and conversion factors.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Make all listed parameters adjustable via software points.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Override Capability for Points
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: All points used in control sequences
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Values for all points, including real (hardware) points used in control sequences, shall be capable of being overridden by the user with appropriate access level (e.g., for testing and commissioning). If hardware design prevents this, equate to a software point and use that in sequences.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarm Levels Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: There shall be 4 levels of alarm: Level 1 (Life-safety), Level 2 (Critical equipment), Level 3 (Urgent), Level 4 (Normal).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Implement four alarm levels as defined.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Maintenance Mode Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operators shall have the ability to put any device in/out of maintenance mode. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed, except life safety alarms. Issue a daily Level 3 alarm if device remains in maintenance mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress all alarms except life safety; issue daily Level 3 alarm at scheduled time indicating device still in maintenance mode.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Operators shall have the ability to put any device (e.g., AHU) in/out of maintenance mode. a. All alarms associated with a device in maintenance mode will be suppressed. Exception: Life safety alarms shall not be suppressed. b. If a device is in maintenance mode, issue a daily Level 3 alarm at a scheduled time indicating that the device is still in maintenance mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarm Exit Hysteresis (Time-Based)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Each alarm shall have an adjustable time-based hysteresis (default: 5 seconds) to exit the alarm. The alarm does not return to normal until the alarm conditions have ceased for the duration of the hysteresis.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Implement adjustable time-based exit hysteresis with default 5 seconds.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VFD Speed Point Configuration
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The speed command point for VFDs should be configured so that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% corresponds to maximum speed set in the VFD, not necessarily 60 Hz.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Configure speed command so 0% = 0 Hz, 100% = maximum speed in VFD.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: To avoid operator confusion, the speed command point (and speed feedback point, if used) for VFDs should be configured so that a speed of 0% corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% corresponds to maximum speed set in the VFD, not necessarily 60 Hz.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VFD Minimum Speed Synchronization
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The minimum speed shall be stored in a single software point. The active minimum speed parameter shall be read every 60 minutes via the drive's network interface. When a mismatch is detected, write the software point value to the VFD and generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Mismatch between drive's active minimum speed and software point minimum speed
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Read drive minimum speed every 60 minutes; if mismatch, write software point value to VFD and generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The active minimum speed parameter shall be read every 60 minutes via the drive's network interface. When a mismatch between the drive's active minimum speed and the minimum speed stored in the software point is detected, the minimum speed stored in the software point shall be written to the VFD via the network interface to restore the active minimum speed parameter to its default value, and generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Runtime Points Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment: Lifetime Runtime (cumulative since startup, not readily resettable) and Staging Runtime (operator resettable).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Define Lifetime Runtime and Staging Runtime points.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Lag Alternation Based on Staging Runtime
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Identical parallel staged equipment (e.g., CHW pumps, cooling towers)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is made last stage and the one with the least hours is made lead stage.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: More than one equipment off or more than one on
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alternate lead/lag based on Staging Runtime: most hours to last stage, least hours to lead stage.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.

evidence_quote: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Standby Alternation for Redundant Equipment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours (Staging Runtime) is made last stage and the one with the least hours is made earlier stage.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Alternate lead/standby based on Staging Runtime.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.

evidence_quote: Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Lead/Standby Switch for Continuously Running Equipment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is shut off.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Switch lead/standby at adjustable day/time; start and prove standby before shutting off former lead.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hand Operation Response for Fans and Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If a piece of equipment is ON-in-hand, the equipment shall be set to the lead device, and a Level 4 alarm shall be generated. The equipment will remain as lead until released.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set equipment to lead device and generate Level 4 alarm; remain lead until released.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If a piece of equipment is ON-in-hand (e.g., via an HOA switch or local control of VFD), the equipment shall be set to the lead device, and a Level 4 alarm shall be generated. The equipment will remain as lead until.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Economizer Disable Based on High Limit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Economizer shall be disabled whenever the outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high-limit setpoint as specified by local code. Setpoints shall be automatically determined by the control sequences based on energy standard, climate zone, and device type.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Outdoor air conditions exceed high-limit setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Disable economizer.
configurable_values: High-limit setpoint (determined automatically based on code, climate zone, device type)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Economizer shall be disabled whenever the outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high-limit setpoint as specified by local code. Setpoints shall be automatically determined by the control sequences (to ensure they are correct and meet code) based on energy standard, climate zone, and economizer high-limit-control device type selected by the design engineer in Section 3.1.4.3 or 3.1.6.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hierarchical Alarm Suppression Principle
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Alarms for load components shall be selectively suppressed if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. Suppression cascades through multiple levels.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress alarms for load components; cascade through levels.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load.

evidence_quote: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load.
evidence_text: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SystemOK Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SystemOK shall be TRUE when the system is proven ON, achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes, and ready and able to serve its load.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SystemOK TRUE when all conditions met.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load

evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load
evidence_text: SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load

structured_payload_candidate.title: SystemOK FALSE Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.summary: SystemOK shall be FALSE while the system is starting up or when enough components are unavailable to disrupt service. By default, Level 1 through Level 3 component alarms inhibit SystemOK; Level 4 alarms do not.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: System starting up or insufficient available components
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set SystemOK FALSE; operator can individually determine which component alarms inhibit SystemOK.
configurable_values: Threshold for number of unavailable components (defined by engineer)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: SystemOK shall be FALSE while the system is starting up (i.e., before reaching setpoint) or when enough of the system's components are unavailable (in alarm, disabled, or turned OFF) to disrupt the ability of the system to serve its load. This threshold shall be defined by the design engineer for each system. a. By default, Level 1 through Level 3 component alarms (indicating equipment failure) shall inhibit SystemOK. Level 4 component alarms (maintenance and energy efficiency alarms) shall not affect SystemOK.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Selective Alarm Suppression by System Type
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system, suppress only high-temperature alarms from loads. If FALSE for heating water system, suppress only low-temperature alarms. If FALSE for air-side system, suppress all alarms from loads.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress specific alarm types based on source system type.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.

evidence_quote: a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.
evidence_text: a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Alarms Never Suppressed by Hierarchical Logic
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Life/safety and Level 1 alarms, failure-to-start alarms, and failure-to-stop/hand alarms will never be suppressed by this logic.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Never suppress these alarm types.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The following types of alarms will never be suppressed by this logic: a. Life/safety and Level 1 alarms b. Failure-to-start alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded ON, but status point shows equipment to be OFF) c. Failure-to-stop/hand alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded OFF, but status point shows equipment to be ON)

evidence_quote: The following types of alarms will never be suppressed by this logic: a. Life/safety and Level 1 alarms b. Failure-to-start alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded ON, but status point shows equipment to be OFF) c. Failure-to-stop/hand alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded OFF, but status point shows equipment to be ON)

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Based Suppression Delay After Setpoint Change
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Calculate a time-delay period after any change in setpoint based on the difference between the controlled variable and the new setpoint. Default: 18 minutes per °C (10 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 120 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply time-delay suppression for alarms and reset requests.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Calculate a time-delay period after any change in setpoint based on the difference between the controlled variable (e.g., zone temperature) at the time of the change and the new setpoint. The default time delay period shall be as follows: a. For thermal zone temperature alarms: 18 minutes per °C (10 minutes per °F) of difference but no longer than 120 minutes
